original:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She loved to walk and listen to her small radio. One sunny day, she decided to walk to the park. She took her radio and walked happily, listening to fun songs.
At the park, Sue met a boy named Tim. He saw her radio and said, "Wow! I like your radio. Can I listen too?" Sue felt a little ashamed because she didn't want to share. But she knew it was nice to share, so she said, "Okay, we can listen together."
Sue and Tim sat under a big tree and listened to the radio. They laughed and clapped as they heard funny songs. Soon, they became good friends. They walked home together, holding hands and singing along to the radio. Sue was happy she shared her radio, and they played together every day after that.
summary: Sue shares her radio with a boy named Tim at the park and they become good friends.

Tim requested. Everyone shared the radio. Sue shared the radio. Sue listened carefully. Sue laughed and laughed. There was clap. A special bond formed between them. She dailied play Tim and the radio.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a wise doctor. He worked in a big hospital. He loved to help people feel better. One day, a little boy came to the hospital. He was very sad because his toy was dirty.
The wise doctor saw the boy and wanted to help. He took the toy and said, "Let's wash it!" The boy watched as the doctor washed the toy with soap and water. The toy became clean and shiny. The boy was so happy to see his toy clean again.
But then, another boy came to the hospital with a dirty toy too. The first boy did not want to share the doctor. He wanted the doctor to only help him. The wise doctor talked to both boys. He said, "We can all be friends and help each other." The boys listened and decided to share. They all washed toys together and became good friends.
summary: A wise doctor helps a sad boy clean his toy and teaches him and another boy about sharing and friendship.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved playing baseball with his friends. He had a favorite bat that he always used. One day, Timmy noticed that his bat was starting to feel weak. It didn't hit the ball as far as it used to.
Timmy's dad saw him struggling with his bat and decided to help. He took Timmy to the store and they picked out a new bat. It was shiny and strong, and Timmy couldn't wait to use it.
The next day, Timmy went to play baseball with his friends. He pulled out his new bat and everyone was impressed. They all wanted to try it out. Timmy felt proud as he pitched the ball and his friends hit it far with his new bat.
From that day on, Timmy's new bat became his favorite. He knew it was strong and wouldn't get weak like his old one. Timmy learned that sometimes it's important to make a change when something isn't working right.
summary: Timmy's old baseball bat started to feel weak, so his dad took him to the store to pick out a new one. Timmy's new bat became his favorite and he learned the importance of making a change when something isn't working right.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a happy crab named Crusty. He lived in a cozy cave by the sea. One day, Crusty found a shiny oyster on the shore. He was so excited and jolly that he danced around with it.
But then, Crusty realized the oyster was dirty and needed to be cleaned. So, he took it back to his cave and scrubbed it with a sponge. After a while, the oyster was shiny and clean again.
Crusty was very proud of himself for being able to clean the oyster. He put it on a rock outside his cave so everyone could see how beautiful it was. From that day on, Crusty always made sure to keep his home and the beach clean.
summary: A happy crab named Crusty found a dirty oyster on the shore, cleaned it, and put it on display outside his cave, inspiring him to keep his home and the beach clean.
generated: Once upon a time, there was a happy proud crab named Crusty. Crusty made a wonderful discovery. Crusty cleaned the oyster. They felt proud. There was the oyster. They felt commitment.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved going to the zoo to see all the animals. One day, Timmy went to the zoo with his mommy and daddy. They walked around and saw lots of animals like monkeys, elephants, and birds. 
Suddenly, Timmy saw a big tiger! The tiger was gray and looked very scary. Timmy's mommy told him to hold her hand tight so he wouldn't get scared. Timmy asked his mommy, "Can we go see the tiger up close?" His mommy said, "No, it's not safe to get too close to the tiger." 
Timmy was sad but he knew his mommy was right. As they walked away, Timmy waved goodbye to the tiger and said, "Bye bye, gray tiger! See you next time!"
summary: Timmy goes to the zoo with his parents and sees lots of animals including a scary-looking tiger. His mommy tells him it's not safe to get too close to the tiger.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a clumsy little bird named Bobo. He wanted to learn how to fly. One day, he saw a big sail in the sky. It was a kite! Bobo thought that the kite could help him fly.
He asked his friend, the wise old owl, "Can you show me how to fly with the sail?" The wise old owl said, "Yes, I can help you. But first, you must sign your name on this paper." Bobo signed his name on the paper with a big, happy smile.
The next day, the wise old owl helped Bobo hold onto the sail. With a big gust of wind, the sail lifted Bobo up into the sky. Bobo was flying! He was so happy that he laughed and laughed. Bobo learned that even clumsy little birds can fly with a little help from their friends.
summary: Bobo, a clumsy little bird, learns to fly with the help of a kite and his wise old owl friend.

In the end, Up into the sky it flew. Bobo felt confident.

kernel:
Lily(Character, child + Brave + Playful)  
Max(Character, child + Brave + Playful)  
Mom(Character, caring + Protective + Warning)  

Cautionary(  
    protagonists = Lily + Max,  
    state = Routine + Longing([Climb(tree), Win]),  
    catalyst = Wind,  
    accident = Fall(Lily + Max, cause = Wind + High(tree)),  
    rescue = Help(Mom, Lily + Max),  
    lesson = Listen(Lily + Max, source = Mom),  
    outcome = Trust(Lily + Max, Mom) + Safety  
)
original:
Lily and Max liked to play in the park. They liked to swing, slide and run. But their favorite thing was to climb the big tree. The big tree had many branches and leaves. It was very high.
One day, they went to the park with their mom. Mom said, "You can play, but be careful. The big tree is very high. You might fall and hurt yourself." Lily and Max nodded, but they did not listen. They ran to the big tree and started to climb.
They climbed higher and higher. They felt happy and brave. They wanted to reach the top. They said to each other, "Who can climb the highest? Who can win?"
But mom saw them and was worried. She called to them, "Lily! Max! Come down! It is too high! You are not safe!" She warned them, but they did not hear. They kept climbing.
Suddenly, a strong wind blew. The big tree shook. The branches swayed. Lily and Max lost their balance. They screamed and grabbed the tree. They were scared. They wished they had listened to mom.
Mom ran to the big tree. She helped them down. She hugged them and said, "Are you okay? I told you it was too high. You could have fallen and hurt yourself. You should listen to me. I love you and I want you to be safe."
Lily and Max felt sorry. They said, "We are sorry, mom. We were silly. We wanted to win. But we did not win. We were scared. We love you too. We will listen to you next time."
Mom smiled and said, "It is okay. I am glad you are okay. You are my victory. You are my precious children. Come on, let's go home and have some cookies."
Lily and Max nodded and smiled. They held mom's hand and walked home. They learned their lesson. They did not want to climb the big tree anymore. They wanted to be safe and happy with mom.
summary: Lily and Max climb a big tree in the park despite their mother's warning, but when a strong wind blows, they lose their balance and their mother helps them down. They learn their lesson and decide to be safe and happy with their mother.
generated: Once upon a time, there was a person named Lily. There was also a person named Max. There was also a person named Mom. Mom went about the day as usual. They really wanted Mom climbed the tree and win. They trusted Lily and Max. Everyone was safe and sound. Mom listened carefully.
notes: 'wanted Mom climbed the tree and win'

kernel:
Lily(Character, girl, Playful + Protective)  
Ben(Character, boy, Curious + Impulsive + Remorseful)  
Mommy(Character, adult, Caring + Forgiving)  

Play(Dolls) + TeaParty(leaves, flowers)  
Discovery(powderJar)  

Accident(Ben,  
    process=Grab(jar) + Open(jar) + Shake(jar),  
    consequence=Mess(powder) + Sneezing + Sadness(Lily) + Fear(Ben))  

Reaction(Lily(angry + sad), Ben(weep + scared))  

Intervention(Mommy,  
    comfort(Ben) + Clean(mess) + Teach(AskFirst) + Encourage(Apology))  

Resolution(Apology(Ben, Lily) + Forgiveness(Lily) + NewTeaParty)  

Cautionary(Ben,  
    event=Accident,  
    lesson=AskFirst + Apology)
original:
Lily and Ben were playing with their dolls in the garden. They liked to dress them up and make them talk. Lily had a pink doll with long hair and Ben had a blue doll with a hat.
"Hello, I am Princess Rose and this is my castle," Lily said, making her doll wave. "Who are you?"
"I am Captain Sky and this is my spaceship," Ben said, making his doll zoom. "I came to visit you from another planet."
They pretended to have a tea party with their dolls, using leaves and flowers as cups and cakes. They were having fun until Ben saw a big jar of powder on the table.
"What is that?" he asked, pointing at the jar.
"That is Mommy's powder. She puts it on her face to make it shiny," Lily said.
"Can I see?" Ben asked, reaching for the jar.
"No, you can't. It is not for playing. It is only for Mommy," Lily said, pulling the jar away.
But Ben did not listen. He grabbed the jar and opened it. He wanted to see what the powder looked like. He shook the jar and a cloud of white powder came out. It covered his face, his hair, his clothes and his doll. He sneezed and coughed.
"Look what you did!" Lily shouted. "You made a mess. You ruined my tea party. You are a bad boy."
She took her doll and ran away. She was angry and sad. Ben felt sorry and scared. He did not mean to make a mess. He did not mean to make Lily angry and sad. He wanted to say sorry, but he did not know how. He started to weep. He wept and wept. His face was wet and his eyes were red.
Mommy heard the noise and came to the garden. She saw Ben weeping and the powder everywhere. She was surprised and worried.
"What happened, Ben?" she asked, hugging him.
"I-I-I p-played with the p-powder and m-made a m-mess and L-Lily got m-mad and r-ran away and I-I-I am s-sorry," Ben sobbed.
Mommy wiped his tears and his nose. She smiled and kissed him.
"It's okay, Ben. It was an accident. You did not mean to make a mess. But you should not play with things that are not yours. You should ask first. And you should say sorry to Lily. She is your sister and she loves you. Do you love her?"
"Yes, I love her," Ben said, nodding.
"Then let's go and find her and make up. Maybe we can clean the mess together and have a real tea party. How does that sound?"
Ben smiled and nodded. He felt better. He took his doll and followed Mommy. He hoped Lily would forgive him. He did not want to play with the powder anymore. It was dull and itchy. He wanted to play with his sister and his doll. They were fun and nice.
summary: Lily and Ben play with their dolls in the garden until Ben accidentally spills Mommy's powder and makes a mess. Lily gets angry and runs away, but Mommy helps Ben apologize and they make up.
generated: Once upon a time, there was a little playful protective girl named Lily. There was also a little curious impulsive boy named Ben. There was also a caring forgiving adult named Mommy. There was dolls. There was the leaves and the flowers. Mommy discovered the powder jar. Suddenly, something went wrong. There was a big mess of the powder. There was sneezing. Lily felt sad. Ben felt scared. The angry and the sad the weep and the scared Mommy stepped in with Ben felt comfort. Ben cleaned the mess. There was askfirst. Ben apologized. Ben said sorry to Lily. Lily chose to forgive. There was new tea party. There was ask first. Ben apologized.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a clever little girl named Lily. She loved to stare at the big, colorful vans that drove by her house every day. One day, as she was staring out the window, she saw a van that looked different from all the others. It was covered in sparkly stickers and had a big sign on the side that said "Free Candy!"
Lily was so excited to see the van that she ran outside to get a closer look. But as she got closer, she realized that something was not right. The driver of the van looked mean and scary, and Lily knew that she should not go near him. She quickly ran back inside her house and told her mom what she had seen.
Her mom was very proud of Lily for being so clever and staying safe. They called the police to report the van, and it turned out that the driver was a bad person who wanted to hurt children. Thanks to Lily's quick thinking, the police were able to catch the bad guy and keep the neighborhood safe. From that day on, Lily continued to stare at the vans that drove by, but she always made sure to stay safe and never went near any that looked suspicious.
summary: Lily sees a van with a sign that says "Free Candy!" but realizes the driver looks scary and tells her mom. They report the van to the police and it turns out the driver was a bad person who wanted to hurt children.

Once upon a time, there was a little frog named Jazz. He loved to jump all day long. One day, Jazz found a new place to play. It was a big pond with many new friends.
Jazz met a fish named Fin. Fin said, "Hi Jazz, do you want to play with me?" Jazz was happy and said, "Yes! Let's jump and have fun!" So, Jazz and Fin played together. They jumped high and low in the pond. They laughed and had a great time.
But then, Jazz saw a big bird in the sky. He was scared and stopped jumping. Fin said, "Don't worry, Jazz. We can be brave together." Jazz and Fin helped each other and stayed close. The big bird flew away, and they were safe.
In the end, Jazz and Fin learned that being brave and helping friends is important. They continued to jump and play in their new pond, always looking out for each other. And they lived happily ever after.

Lily said to her mom, "I want to find a way to stop the hurricane." Her mom was surprised, but she knew Lily was brave. She said, "Be careful, Lily."
Lily went outside and saw a big tree. The tree was sad because the hurricane was hurting it. Lily hugged the tree and said, "Don't worry, I will help you." Suddenly, the hurricane stopped. Once upon a time, there was a smart dog named Max. Max loved to play fetch with his owner, Tim. One day, Tim went to get the newspaper and Max reminded him to bring it back. Tim smiled and said, "You're a smart dog, Max!"
Later that day, Max saw Tim reading the newspaper. Max wanted to see what was so interesting, so he jumped up and tried to grab it. Tim laughed and said, "Sorry Max, this newspaper is for grown-ups. But don't worry, I'll read you a story from your favorite book instead."
Max wagged his tail happily and snuggled up next to Tim as he read. Max loved story time with Tim, and he was glad Tim had reminded him that there were other fun things to do besides play fetch.

Lily loved to play with her toys, but sometimes she would stretch them too much and they would break. Her mom would tell her to be careful and not be so rough with her toys. But one day, Lily saw her little brother playing with her favorite toy. She got very angry and wanted to take it away from him. Her mom told her that it was naughty to be so selfish and that she should share her toys with her brother.
From that day on, Lily learned to be more careful with her toys and to share them with her brother. She also learned that lightning is a natural part of the world and that there's no need to be afraid of it. The end.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing in the forest near her house. One day, she saw a big log and decided to play on it. But the log was too noisy and scared away all the animals.
Lily's friend, a little bird, came to her and said, "Please, Lily, can you play somewhere else? The log is too noisy and it hurts my ears."
Lily didn't want to leave the log, so she refused her friend's request. She continued to play on the log, making lots of noise.
Suddenly, the log broke and Lily fell down. She hurt her leg and couldn't walk. She realized that her friend was right, and she should have listened to the request. From that day on, Lily always listened to her friends and never played on noisy logs again.
summary: Lily plays on a noisy log despite her friend's request, but eventually falls and learns to listen to her friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat ice cream with a spoon. One day, she dropped her spoon on the ground and it got all dirty. 
Lily's mom said, "Oh no! Your spoon is bad now. Let's go wash it." 
Lily replied, "Thank you, Mommy. I love my spoon." 
After they washed the spoon, Lily happily ate her ice cream again. She said, "My spoon is clean now. Yummy!" 
Her mom smiled and said, "I'm glad you like your spoon, Lily. It's important to take care of our things." 
Lily nodded and said, "Yes, thank you, Mommy."
summary: Lily dropped her ice cream spoon and her mom washed it for her, reminding her to take care of her things.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Tabby. She found some coal on the ground and decided to try chewing on it. Tabby's mum saw her and said, in a shocked voice, "Tabby! What are you doing?! Coal is not something to chew!" Tabby looked up, her mouth filled with coal, and said, "But it's so pretty!" Her mum smiled and replied, "It may be attractive, but it's not something to chew. It's not good for you!" Tabby nodded and decided to use the coal to make something else. She grabbed the coal, put it in her pocket and ran off to find some water and a stone.
Once Tabby had the water, stone and coal, she placed the coal on the stone, with a bit of the water. She then took the stone and pounded it with her little hand. When she finished, she found she had made a very attractive "coal sparkle". She smiled and said, proudly, "I made something wonderful with the coal!"

original:
One day, Joe and his mum were in the playroom. Joe saw a colourful folder sitting on the table.
Joe: "Mum, can I play with that folder?"
Mum: "Yes, but only if you promise to be careful. It has all of my perfect papers in it and I don't want them to get crumpled."
Joe took the folder and opened it. Inside were lots of colourful paper. He carefully took a piece out, folded it, rolled it, and started playing a game. Joe put it back in the folder, then took out another piece of paper to play with.
Suddenly, Joe heard his mum call his name. She said it was time to quit the game and put the folder away. Joe was reluctant, but he put the folder back in its place.
Mum smiled at Joe and said, "Good job! Now, let's do something else!" Joe nodded with a smile and followed his Mum out of the room, feeling proud of himself for doing such a perfect job.
summary: Joe plays with his mum's colourful folder but is careful not to crumple the papers inside. His mum praises him for doing a good job and they move on to something else.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a big armchair in a small room. The armchair was very high and soft. A little boy named Tim lived in the house with the armchair. Tim loved to sit in the armchair and read his books.
One day, Tim's friend, Sue, came over to play. They played with their toys and had lots of fun. Then, Sue saw the high armchair and wanted to sit in it. She asked Tim, "Can I sit in the armchair?"
Tim thought for a moment and said, "Yes, but be careful. It is very high!" Sue climbed up and sat in the armchair. She felt very happy and tall. They both took turns sitting in the armchair and felt like they belonged there.
After a while, it was time for Sue to go home. She said goodbye to Tim and the armchair. Tim waved goodbye to Sue and climbed back into the high armchair. He knew that the armchair was special and that it belonged to him and his friends.
summary: Tim and his friend Sue had fun playing with their toys. Sue saw Tim's high armchair and wanted to sit in it. They both took turns sitting in the armchair and felt like they belonged there.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a great horse named Buddy. Buddy lived on a farm with his friends. He liked to run and play with them. Buddy had a dream. He wanted to learn how to fly.
One day, Buddy saw a big bird in the sky. He thought, "If the bird can fly, maybe I can too!" Buddy tried to jump and flap his legs, but he could not fly. He felt sad.
Then, something unexpected happened. A kind wind blew and lifted Buddy up into the sky. Buddy was flying! He was so happy. The wind was his friend and helped him fly. Buddy learned that with a little help, dreams can come true.
summary: Buddy, a horse, dreams of flying and tries to fly like a bird but fails. However, with the help of a kind wind, he is able to fly and learns that dreams can come true with a little help.

An intelligent bird saw Tim and flew down to talk. The bird said, "Hi, Tim! That cross is a sign for something. You should not go there." But Tim did not listen to the bird.
Tim walked to the cross and fell into a big hole. The bird looked at Tim and said, "I told you not to go there." Tim was sad because he did not listen to the intelligent bird.

Once upon a time, there was a big lion. He lived in a jungle. The lion was very strong and he could roar very loud. One day, the lion was walking in the jungle and he found a shiny gem. The gem was very pretty and it sparkled in the sun. The lion picked up the gem and put it in his mouth. He walked around with the gem in his mouth and felt very happy.
As the sun started to set, the jungle became very dark. The lion was still walking with the gem in his mouth. Suddenly, he saw a group of animals. They were scared of the lion because he was big and strong. But the lion didn't want to hurt them. He just wanted to show them his gem. The animals saw the shiny gem and they were amazed. They had never seen anything so pretty before. The lion felt very proud and happy to share his gem with his new friends.
summary: A strong lion finds a shiny gem in the jungle and shares it with a group of scared animals, making them amazed and happy.

original:
Once upon a time, there was a yellow cat named Sunny. Sunny lived in a small house with his friend, a girl named Lily. They liked to play games and solve puzzles together. Sunny was very good at finding things with his sharp eyes.
One day, Lily found a tiny, little thing on the floor. She could not see it well, so she used a tool called a microscope. The microscope made the tiny thing look big. Lily and Sunny looked at it together.
They saw that it was a piece of a puzzle. They wanted to solve the puzzle, so they searched the house for more pieces. They looked under the bed, behind the door, and in the toy box. Finally, they found all the pieces and put the puzzle together. It was a picture of a big, yellow sun, just like Sunny the cat. They were so happy and proud that they solved the puzzle together.
summary: Sunny the yellow cat and his friend Lily found a puzzle piece and searched the house for more pieces to solve the puzzle together, which turned out to be a picture of a big, yellow sun like Sunny.

In the end, there was the sun. Sunny felt full of joy. They felt proud.
